title: Hard-coded secrets are security-sensitive
description: This rule detects keys having a name matching a list of words (secret, token, credential, auth, api[_.-]?key) being assigned a pseudorandom hard-coded value. The pseudorandomness of the hard-coded value is based on its entropy and the probability to be human-readable. The randomness sensibility can be adjusted if needed.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx-app
spec:
  containers:
    - name: nginx
      image: "nginx:1.21.6"
      ports:
        - containerPort: 80
      env:
        - name: API_TOKEN
          value: "f7a9s8d7f6as98df7a6s9d8f7a6sd9f87as6df"  # Sensitive
compliant:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx-app
spec:
  containers:
    - name: nginx
      image: "nginx:1.21.6"
      ports:
        - containerPort: 80
      env:
        - name: API_TOKEN
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: my-secret
              key: api-key
